re-measured at HEAD (b7a239b, always-idle reopen); record status=idle proposal cleared on :8803
guarded demote: working->idle on :8805
resume of a review session keeps awaiting/merge (record ground truth) on :8805
working session resume -> idle/proposal='' on :8807 HEAD
working session resume -> idle on :8807 HEAD
online-path isolation: POST /review then /resume keeps awaiting/merge (record) on :8807 HEAD
Measured on the real CLI: session done --propose close output carries the plain project-agnostic cleanup reminder; --propose merge does not; no repo path leaked.

Governed session via real POST /api/sessions on isolated :8833 backend (clone repo, SPEXCODE_HOME + SPEXCODE_TMUX isolated, stub launcher); after a UserPromptSubmit round-trip the worktree holds ZERO per-session files (no .session/, git status clean — only project-level materialized shims .claude/.codex/AGENTS-block); record+launch.sh+prompt live under $SPEXCODE_HOME/projects/<enc>/sessions/<id>/.

Two agents (distinct payload session_ids) fired mark-active concurrently in ONE worktree: X→active, Y→asking with its own question note; two distinct global records, neither clobbered the other nor the host session's record.

Stop for governed:false record AND for no-record-at-all → exit 0, empty stdout/stderr (no declare demand). Stop for governed undeclared+dirty → decision:block with the declare-gate reason, dispatch exit 2. The governed flag alone flips it.

Recordless (self-launched) agent: first code Read blocked once by spec-first (sentinel created on demand in the global session dir, no session.json), second Read passed silently. spec-of-file also ran ungated: first edit of an uncovered file emitted the additionalContext annotation, second deduped via ledger (sanely-owned files stay silent by --actionable design).

Full governed loop on :8833: prompt→active; AskUserQuestion→asking+question note; next tool→active (stale note dropped); session done --propose merge→awaiting/merge with the recorded confirmation echo; declared+committed Stop passed silently; next tool re-flipped active and dropped the proposal. Board row composes lifecycle×liveness (a dead asking still reads asking). Behavior identical, storage global.

GET /api/sessions listed exactly the governed records from $SPEXCODE_HOME/projects/<enc>/sessions/* ordered by createdAt; a governed:false record and two sentinel-only dirs never appeared; POST /:id/close removed the record dir AND the worktree and the row vanished.

POST /:id/exit killed tmux, record survived untouched (active); POST /:id/resume relaunched the agent and settled active→idle with proposal '' — no phantom working before the next human prompt. Isolated :8833 backend at HEAD.

Online-path isolation on a LIVE paused agent: set awaiting/merge via session done --propose merge, then POST /resume (no relaunch, no mark-active) — record stayed awaiting with proposal merge intact; reopen never touched the proposal field.

Real CLI echoes: --propose close carries the advisory project-agnostic cleanup reminder (background process / dev server / bound port / scratch session; keyed on should-it-outlive-the-task, 'If unsure, leave it', explicitly a reminder not a gate, zero repo paths); --propose merge and bare done do NOT carry it.

Listener-verify (tooth 2): stub session with a tmux window + live rendezvous LISTENER reads online; killing the listener (stale socket file present) → listSessions liveness reads offline within seconds. Proven via isolated repro (SPEXCODE_HOME+SPEXCODE_TMUX) driving real listSessions(), plus unit tests (claude liveness socketLive gate; rendezvousListening stale-file→false).

Resume guard (tooth 3): reopen() on a LIVE stub (fresh listener-verified liveness) returns {ok:false,refused:true} with a loud message; the live worker is NOT killed (tmux window still present after the refused reopen). API maps refused→409; --force + merge(guard:false) exempt. Proven via isolated repro driving real reopen().

Board honesty (tooth 1): unit-level probe-timeout injection — liveness(rec,{probeFailed:true})='unknown' (row present, never a false offline); a clean empty probe stays authoritative 'offline'. tmux probe bounded by TMUX_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS so a hung tmux can't freeze/vanish the list; probeTimedOut tells a kill from a clean no-server exit.

Re-measured at the closeout-nudge change on the real CLI: --propose close still carries the advisory project-agnostic cleanup reminder verbatim (reminder-not-a-gate wording, zero repo paths), now followed by the data-driven issue-closeout line only when the session owes open issues; --propose merge and bare done carry neither.
A repro: done --note silently dropped (record note stays empty); ask/park record fully but the echo says nothing while spex ls truncates NOTE at 50 chars — truncation invisible to the author

B: done/ask/park --note all reach the record in full; a >50-char note's echo states N chars / board shows first 50 / where the full text is readable (spex review <id>, spex ls --json); short or absent notes get no line; declaration always lands (nudge, not gate)

Re-measured on throwaway backend (isolated SPEXCODE_HOME, stub launcher). done/ask/park each store a 212-char note IN FULL in session.json (byte-equal); each long declaration echoes the transparency line (length 212, board shows first 50, readable via spex review <id> / spex ls --json); spex ls NOTE column truncates at 50 with ellipsis; a short note gets no transparency line; declaration lands regardless.

REGRESSION: the bounded-probe half FAILS. Repro (real backend, real tmux): SIGSTOP the tmux server (a genuinely wedged tmux) -> GET /api/sessions AND /api/board FREEZE indefinitely (>120s; curl gave up at 30s/15s) — the frozen-list state the expected forbids, and liveness 'unknown' never renders because assembly never completes. Root cause visible in the process table: liveSnapshot's list-sessions IS bounded (4s, clients SIGKILLed) but paneTitles() calls tmux list-panes with NO timeout (sessions.ts tmux() called without timeoutMs), so listSessions' Promise.all never resolves and stuck list-panes clients pile up. Contrast leg PASSES: after tmux kill-server (clean empty server) the board answers in 0.1s, all 13 rows persist, liveness authoritative offline (fresh launches read starting through boot grace, then offline) — never unknown, never vanished. Fix hint: pass TMUX_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS to the paneTitles/sessionSignature tmux calls.

B leg re-measured (A leg is the historical pre-fix existsSync behaviour). Stub governed claude (real listener on the rendezvous socket) read working/online. SIGKILL'd the claude stand-in at 01:53:19.146 leaving the socket FILE on disk AND the tmux pane/wrapper alive; the very next board read at 01:53:19.463 (~300ms) returned liveness=offline. The stale socket file was demonstrably present at read time — connect() refusal, not file existence, is what decided. Session launched >45s prior so boot grace was not masking.

All four legs through the real API. (1) POST resume on an ALIVE agent (live listener) -> HTTP 409 {refused:true} with the loud steer-by-message error; listener pid and pane pid untouched. (2) liveness UNPROVEN (SIGSTOP'd the real tmux server -> probe fails) -> 409 refused with the probe-failed wording. (3) merge dispatch (guard:false) on the same live agent -> 200 {dispatched:true}, pane pid unchanged (reused, no refusal, no relaunch). (4) force:true -> 200, old listener killed, fresh pane+listener, back online; declared lifecycle (awaiting) survived the relaunch. A leg historical (pre-fix silent kill).
